I have finally linked 2-3-74 totally with an extant philosohy and philosopher. What I saw-- pretextual cause, Valis, [MIT] motion, linking and relinking, Valis, mind, the dialectic, unity instead of plurality, etc-- oh yes: the [mmsk], and of course the meta-abstraction in which USA 1974 and acts superimposed syntinically, and [xtian] apocalyptic history- 15 the philosophy of Nicholas de Malebranche; mainly what I saw is his, "occasionalism" and this explains my [niginal] insight into the irreality of causation back in the 1403. Malebranche's philosophy is very close to spinoza's, as Durant points out, and also is related to Plato and neoplatonism by way of Augustine. The "clutch" system. One thinks that reality is acting on him, but it is really God. 3-74 was an apperception of God as the true basis of all reality, and perception as being and eternal ideal in the mind of God (the [mmiki]). To what do I owe this 3-74 vision of God and the mere occasionality of world? The answer is contained in Malebranche system based on Augustine): in God himself. God as the true cause of everything (that happens) [make] the whole thing (world) seem real.

I What I find in Malebranche is two elements that apply to 3-74: 1) His critique of causation. -his doctrine of occasionalism- that fits in with what call "pretextual cause" + was a major

perception in 3-74. This is connected with the inner-outer tape synch + engramming. 2) His "vision in God." We do not in fact experience world but archetypal intelligible ideas in God's mind. This would explain the meta-abstraction of 2-74 + the elements connected with it; e.g. the syntonic double superimposition of two spatiotemporal sets" revealing to me that in some very major sense world was not world at all, + that the true arrangement was abstract + conceptual. underlying (1) + (2) is: God is the case, not world. There is no world, at least insotar as we are concerned; there are intelligible ideas - more, there is a mens Dee that causes us to experience these ideas as world, + this mens dee signals to us ineluctably through + by means of this seeming world, with the result that we are in effect modes of this mens dei forming one continuum or field with the ideas. The whole thing is one sentient volitional structure with a single underlying mind. Multiplicity is illusory.

There is total, perfect mystical union with God. God acts on us, in us + for us. We do not react to reality; we react to God expressing himself through, in + as reality more‐ over, our reaction is him in us, or due to him - it is the same: inner-outer synch
